docs.title: Welcome to MQP Qiskit Provider Documentation
docs.text: This is the documentation for the MQP Qiskit Provider.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: To get started with the MQP Qiskit Provider, refer to the Getting Started guide.

docs.title: Development Guide
docs.text: For information on setting up the development environment and contributing to the project, refer to the Development Guide.

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.text:
Detailed API documentation for the MQP Qiskit Provider:

  • MQPProvider: The main entry point for accessing MQP backends.
  • MQPBackend: Interfaces with the MQP backends.
  • MQPJob: Manages job execution and results retrieval.

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text: See the CHANGELOG for details on changes in each version.

docs.title: Version 0.1.7
docs.text:
• Fixed KeyError bug when getting target instructions for a resource
  • Add contact us page to docs

docs.title: Version 0.1.6
docs.text:
• Added the option to queue a job when backend is offline
  • Added some more instructions to target as new backends are integrated

docs.title: Version 0.1.5
docs.text:
• Added qasm3 flag to the backend to send circuit as QASM3 when set
  • Added a property num_pending_jobs to the backend to get the num pending jobs

docs.title: Version 0.1.4
docs.text:
• Added the online-backends flag to the backends member of the MQPProvider to fetch only the online devices
  • timestamps (submitted, scheduled and completed) are added to the results. It can be accessed as result.timestamps["submitted"].

docs.title: Contact Us
docs.text: If you encounter any bugs or issues, please let us know by opening a new issue.

docs.title: MQPBackend
docs.text:
Bases: BackendV2

MQP Backend class: This class extends Qiskit's BackendV2 class and provides methods to compile and run circuits on the backend. Users do not need to create an instance of this class directly; it is created and returned by the MQPProvider when a backend is requested.

docs.title: MQPJob
docs.text:
Bases: JobV1

MQPJob Class: This class is used to manage jobs. Users do not need to create an instance of this class directly; it is created and returned by the MQPBackend when a job is submitted via MQPBackend.run.

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
This project provides a Qiskit provider for MQP, allowing users to access MQP backends through Qiskit. The main components of the project are:

  • provider.py: Contains the MQPProvider class, which serves as the main entry point for accessing MQP backends.
  • backend.py: Contains the MQPBackend class, which interfaces with the MQP backends.
  • job.py: Contains the MQPJob class, which handles job cancellation, status checking, and result retrieval.
  • mqp_resource.py: Contains functions to retrieve the coupling map and target for the MQP backend.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you start developing, ensure you have the following installed:

  • Python 3.8 to 3.11
  • uv package manager for python

docs.title: Setting Up the Development Environment
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/Munich-Quantum-Software-Stack/MQP-Qiskit-Provider.git
cd MQP-Qiskit-Provider

Create a virtual environment and install the dependencies:

pdm install

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
To run the tests, use pytest:

pdm run pytest

docs.title: Publishing Documentation on GitHub Pages
docs.text:
To publish the documentation on GitHub Pages, follow these steps:

Install MkDocs and the Material theme:

pdm install -G docs

Build the documentation:

pdm run mkdocs build

View documentation locally

Run the following and browse the documentation locally at: http://localhost:8000

pdm run mkdocs serve

Deploy the documentation to GitHub Pages:

pdm run mkdocs gh-deploy --remote-name git@github.com:Munich-Quantum-Software-Stack/MQP-Qiskit-Provider-Documentation.git --remote-branch gh-pages

This will create a new branch named gh-pages in your repository and deploy the documentation to GitHub Pages.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install the MQP Qiskit provider, use the following command:

pip install mqp-qiskit-provider

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Qiskit provider for MQP allows users to access MQP backends through Qiskit. The main components are:

  • MQPProvider: This is the main entry point for accessing MQP backends.
  • MQPBackend: It interfaces with the MQP backends.
  • MQPJob: It handles job submission, cancellation, status checking, and result retrieval.

docs.title: Import Required Modules
docs.text:
Import the necessary components from Qiskit and the MQP provider package.

from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from mqp.qiskit_provider import MQPProvider

docs.title: Initialize the MQP Provider
docs.text:
Create an instance of MQPProvider by supplying your token from MQP.

provider = MQPProvider(token="<api-token>")

docs.title: List Available Backends
docs.text:
Retrieve and inspect the list of backends accessible to your account.

all_backends = provider.backends()
print("Available backends:", all_backends)

docs.title: List Online Backends
docs.text:
Retrieve and inspect the list of backends that are online and accessible to your account.

all_backends = provider.backends(online=True)
print("Available backends:", all_backends)

docs.title: Select a Specific Backend
docs.text:
Choose a backend by its identifier. Replace <resource-name> with the actual name of the backend.

backend = provider.get_backend("<resource-name>")
# OR
[backend] = provider.backends(name="<resource-name>")

docs.title: Design Your Quantum Circuit
docs.text:
Construct your quantum circuit using Qiskit's QuantumCircuit. For example, create a circuit with 2 qubits and 2 classical bits.

circuit = QuantumCircuit(2, 2)
circuit.h(0)
circuit.cx(0, 1)
circuit.measure([0, 1], [0, 1])

docs.title: Submit Your Job
docs.text:
Submit the circuit for execution on the chosen backend. Specify the number of shots as required. Note that the circuit is transpiled automatically at server before it is submitted to the backend.

job = backend.run(circuit, shots=1000)

docs.title: Transpile Circuit and Submit Job
docs.text:
If you want to transpile the circuit yourself you can do it as follows and then submit it as a job with no_modify=True flag to skip transpilation at server side and submit the circuit as it is to the backend.

The transpilation at user end may not be supported for some backends.

from qiskit import QuantumCircuit, compiler
transpiled_circuit = compiler.transpile(circuit, backend, optimization_level=3)
job = backend.run(transpiled_circuit, shots=1000, no_modify=True)

docs.title: Checking the Number of Pending Jobs on a Backend
docs.text:
To determine if a backend is overloaded, you can check the number of pending jobs using the num_pending_jobs property.

print("Number of pending jobs:", backend.num_pending_jobs)

docs.title: Queuing a Job when Backend is Offline
docs.text:
By default, a job scheduled on a backend with an offline status will be cancelled. To queue a job on an offline backend, use the queued=True flag. This will ensure that the job is enqueued and will be executed once the backend becomes available.

The job is queued for limited time and cancelled if the backend is still offline.

job = backend.run(circuit, shots=1000, queued=True)

docs.title: Monitor Job Status
docs.text:
You can check the status of your job as follows. The status indicates the current stage (e.g., QUEUED, INITIALIZING, DONE).

status = job.status()
print("Job status:", status)

docs.title: Cancel the Job
docs.text:
If necessary, you cancel the job before it completes.

job.cancel()

docs.title: Retrieve and Display Results
docs.text:
Once the job is completed, fetch the results and display the measurement counts.

result = job.result()
counts = result.get_counts()
print("Measurement counts:", counts)

docs.title: Timestamps for Job Execution
docs.text:
result_dict = job.result().to_dict()
print(result_dict["timestamps"]["submitted"])
print(result_dict["timestamps"]["scheduled"])
print(result_dict["timestamps"]["completed"])
